title: The Moon's the North Wind's Cooky
content:
(Moon Poems for the Children/Fairy-tales for the Children)
             (What the Little Girl Said)


         The Moon's the North Wind's cooky.
         He bites it, day by day,
         Until there's but a rim of scraps
         That crumble all away.

         The South Wind is a baker.
         He kneads clouds in his den,
         And bakes a crisp new moon that... greedy
         North... Wind... eats... again!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Vachel Lindsay delivers a powerful performance in "The Moon's the North Wind's Cooky"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
